Regulator_Type.reason: The paper explicitly states that IbbHLH25 encodes a basic helix–loop–helix transcription factor.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results state IbbHLH25 is a bHLH TF, contains a conserved bHLH domain, and is closest to AtbHLH25 of clade IVa.

Source_Species.reason: The study is on sweet potato and IbbHLH25 was cloned from a sweet potato variety.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The organism is sweet potato, but the Latin binomial Ipomoea batatas is not explicitly written in the provided text.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The paper states that IbbHLH25 was cloned from the orange-fleshed variety Pushu32; promoter regions were sequenced in Pushu32 and Xushu18, and haplotypes were analysed in 304 accessions.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional validation was performed in transgenic sweet potato plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species is sweet potato, but the exact Latin name Ipomoea batatas is not explicitly shown in the text provided.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The paper explicitly says Lizixiang was used for the functional characterization of IbbHLH25.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results show IbbHLH25 is highly expressed in storage roots, peaks at 20 DAP, and is more highly expressed in carotenoid-rich varieties than in low-carotenoid varieties.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Figure 2 and Results state highest expression in storage roots of 3-month-old plants and higher expression in carotenoid-rich storage roots.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The IbbHLH25-GFP fusion localized to the nucleus in N. benthamiana cells.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The Results directly quantify total carotenoids, β-carotene, and β-cryptoxanthin changes in IbbHLH25 transgenic roots.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These products are explicitly carotenoids throughout the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Discussion states β-carotene is a vitamin A precursor and carotenoids/β-cryptoxanthin have antioxidant, immunomodulatory, and anti-inflammatory functions.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Carotenoids were measured in storage roots, and carotenoid globules in chromoplasts/storage-root cells were described.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Overexpression increased carotenoid accumulation and increased proportions of β-carotene and β-cryptoxanthin, while RNAi had opposite effects.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four summarized points are directly supported by Results: promoter indel association and promoter activity, OE/RNAi metabolic effects, direct targeting of IbGGPPS/IbLCYB/IbBCH, and heterodimerization/synergy with IbbHLH66.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Each listed method appears in the Results/Methods and was used for IbbHLH25 characterization.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: ChIP-qPCR, EMSA, and dual-LUC support direct targeting of IbGGPPS, IbLCYB, and IbBCH.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Results show direct activation of IbGGPPS, IbLCYB, and IbBCH, and broader upregulation of carotenoid pathway genes in OE lines.

Regulation_Mode.reason: IbbHLH25 is shown to act as a transactivator and activate target promoters.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: The paper directly links IbbHLH25 to carotenoid biosynthesis and altered β-carotene/β-cryptoxanthin proportions.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Discussion explicitly calls IbbHLH25 a core regulatory factor in carotenoid biosynthesis, and Results show strong positive effects on carotenoid accumulation/composition.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Y2H screening identified IbbHLH66 and IbMYB73 as putative interactors; the paper also notes IbMYB73 OE caused no visible flesh-colour change.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: BiFC, LCI, co-IP, EMSA, and dual-LUC support formation and synergistic function of the IbbHLH25–IbbHLH66 heterodimer.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Results identify an 86-bp promoter insertion/deletion affecting promoter activity and a promoter SNP genotype associated with carotenoid content.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The study explicitly references field-grown plants, 20 DAP developmental expression, and harvest at 120 DAP for transgenic analyses.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 35S-driven constructs were used for overexpression/GFP/ChIP; native IbbHLH25 promoters were tested in reporter assays.

Omics_Data.reason: The exact public resource identifiers and resequencing/haplotype source are listed in the Data availability and Results sections.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Figure S4/Results report decreased dry matter and starch and increased soluble sugars in IbbHLH25-OE plants with the stated percentage ranges.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion explicitly proposes the 86-bp variation as a molecular marker and suggests promoter editing/expression enhancement of IbbHLH25, including with IbbHLH66, for carotenoid improvement.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results state IbNAC86 lacked genetic association, identify CACGTG/CATGTG binding sites, and show the N-terminal 85 aa are critical for transactivation.

Regulator_Type.reason: IbbHLH66 is explicitly described as a bHLH protein and functions as a transcriptional regulator of carotenoid genes.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper explicitly states that IbbHLH66 is also a bHLH protein.

Source_Species.reason: IbbHLH66 is studied in sweet potato.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Sweet potato is clear, but the exact Latin name Ipomoea batatas is not explicitly written in the provided text.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional assays were conducted in transgenic sweet potato plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The exact Latin name is not explicitly present in the provided paper text.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The Results state IbbHLH66 overexpression lines were generated using the Lizixiang variety.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The statement is cautious and matches the paper: detailed endogenous expression pattern is not presented here, and function was assessed in storage roots of transgenic plants.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results directly quantify total carotenoids, β-carotene, and β-cryptoxanthin in IbbHLH66-OE storage roots.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Discussion describes β-carotene as provitamin A and carotenoids/β-cryptoxanthin as health-promoting antioxidants.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Measurements were made in storage roots, and carotenoid globules were observed there; carotenoids are noted as stored in chromoplasts.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Overexpression shifted flesh toward orange and increased carotenoid content and the proportions of β-carotene and β-cryptoxanthin.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each statement is supported by Results: interaction with IbbHLH25, OE metabolic increases, direct activation of IbGGPPS/IbLCYB/IbBCH at the same sites, and synergistic co-expression effects.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used for IbbHLH66 in the paper or in direct relation to its interaction/function.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: ChIP-qPCR, EMSA, and dual-LUC support IbGGPPS, IbLCYB, and IbBCH as direct targets.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Results show direct activation of these three genes and broader upregulation of nearly all tested carotenoid pathway genes in OE plants.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Dual-LUC and related assays show transcriptional activation by IbbHLH66.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: The Results directly connect IbbHLH66 to carotenoid biosynthesis.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Results show IbbHLH66 positively regulates carotenoid accumulation both alone and synergistically with IbbHLH25.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: BiFC, LCI, co-IP, and Y2H support interaction with IbbHLH25.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: The paper explicitly shows an IbbHLH25–IbbHLH66 heterodimer with synergistic promoter activation.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The paper states plants were transplanted to the field and harvested at 120 DAP for IbbHLH66 analyses.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 35S:IbbHLH66-GFP is explicitly stated for ChIP, but the specific promoter driving the overexpression lines is not clearly stated in the provided text for IbbHLH66, though likely from prior work.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results/Figure S8 report increased dry matter, starch, and soluble sugar with the stated percentage ranges in IbbHLH66-OE plants.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion states IbbHLH66 is a valuable candidate gene and highlights its potential to balance and enhance quality traits, supporting biofortification utility.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results explicitly state IbbHLH66 targets the same promoter binding sites as IbbHLH25, and no RNAi/knockout analysis for IbbHLH66 is reported in this paper.
